mnemonics: Pam's Dog Barks Seasonally (PDBS): Remember the four components to specify a general Seasonal ARIMA model - (P, D, Q) for the seasonal part and (p, d, q) for the non-seasonal part.
keyPoints: A general multiplicative Seasonal ARIMA model is denoted as ARIMA(p,d,q)×(P,D,Q)s, where 's' is the seasonal period.
The model incorporates both non-seasonal (p,d,q) and seasonal (P,D,Q) components, which are multiplied together in the model equation.
Differencing can be applied to both the non-seasonal (d) and seasonal (D) parts to achieve stationarity.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to apply both seasonal and non-seasonal differencing when necessary, leading to a non-stationary series.
Incorrectly specifying the seasonal lag 's', which is critical for identifying the pattern (e.g., s=12 for monthly data, s=4 for quarterly).
